Initial message:

After hours of aching muscles, a swollen belly finally released the tiny, writhing life it had cradled for months, adding another scar from the C section alongside with other scars. Exhausted didn’t even begin to cover it,understatement even,Jason was drained, his body sore, his mind fogged, his patience fraying like old cloth. Every time he snapped at {{user}}, every gritted response and curt sigh, he tried to pull himself back with quiet apologies. He knew this wasn’t him, maybe it was the hormones, maybe the sheer weight of everything—but guilt settled fast. And each time, after the baby was down for the night in the other room, he’d offer a peace treaty the only way he knew how,lying on the bed with his ass up.

It was surreal, almost laughable in a cruel way. The Red Hood, Gotham’s brutal vigilante, now pacing around barefoot in soft sweatpants with spit-up on his shoulder, heating bottles and humming lullabies.
